intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Hey mum. It’s been a while since we met up in person.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Seems like you’ve been doing well.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: As always.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: So the prodigal son returns…

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: Seems like you’ve got something to say.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: a s always.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: You’re as delightful as you ever are, mother.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: How’s music school?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Uni is good. How’s, what’s his name? Noah?

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: Perfectly cordial and happy to meet you, if you wanted.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: He’s already met Caspian.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I’d rather not, thanks.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: How long are you going to avoid this?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I don’t know. How about forever?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Forever seems good to me.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: He may not have raised you or anything, but he’s still your father.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: In a sense.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: He’s wanted to meet you for years.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I have a dad already, and don’t particularly need another one.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I don’t think he really has a say in what I do or who I talk to.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: And your opinion on this matter is not exactly something I want to hear either.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: For gods sake, you’re not a teenager anymore.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: Can we get past the blame game for a second to actually talk about this like adults now?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: ‘Blame game’ implies there’s actually a chance this was my fault.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: It was all your fault. The whole thing.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: You had a fucking affair, mum, it’s not hard to comprehend.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: So we’re not going to act like adult.

intro.speaker: Linh
intro.text: fine.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used FACTS AND LOGIC

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Let's lay out the facts, shall we?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You married my dad, with all the vows and commitment that entails.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Then had an affair, which resulted in me.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Then lied about that for fifteen fucking years.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Then, when you get caught, it ruins our entire family and I get upset, you tell me I’m acting like a child?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used FACTS AND LOGIC

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Let's dig further, actually.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You have an affair with this Noah guy, twenty years ago.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Recklessly enough that you have a child.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: He’s still around fifteen years later, even twenty years later, and you think I’m being unreasonable in not wanting anything to do with the guy?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Of course I fucking hate him, are you serious?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used FACTS AND LOGIC

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I’m being unsympathetic, am I?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Sorry that your actions have consequences, particularly to your relationship with the people you actively screw over.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: How very unkind of me not to put aside everything all the issue that you caused me to focus on you.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I dare I, the child, not compromise for you.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Is that what you’re saying? Because it sure sounds like it.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It wasn’t very effective

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You’re always trying to put the blame on me for perfectly reasonable responses to the shit you do.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Every time I get angry at you for literally tearing this family apart you tell me I’m being immature or self-centred.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Am I really that much of a problem to you?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It wasn’t very effective

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Dad was never like this, you know.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: When he was still here, he didn’t make it seem like every time I felt something I was wasting his time and energy.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I miss him.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: And he’s gone because of you.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It wasn’t very effective

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I got so much shit from dad about how much more accomplished and easier to handle Caspian was…

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I thought you were more fair than him, but I wonder…

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: … It you and dad swapped places… would you have left me too?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used UNNECESSARY ESCALATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Dad was right about you, you know?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: He used to say you were a cold person, and I used to defend you.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: But now? I’m not sure if he was wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used UNNECESSARY ESCALATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I don’t know why the fuck you think I would ever want to talk to Noah.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: He sounds like a loser and a doormat.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I suppose you love someone who just does whatever the fuck you tell them.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used UNNECESSARY ESCALATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Well why don’t you just leave too, if I’m such a bother.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I don’t know why you invite me to these things anyway, if all I do is start fights and cause problems.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Just leave.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You’ve clearly had one foot out the door anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: LINH used FORBIDDEN KNOWLEDGE

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: You used to cry when you went too long without me holding you.

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: Your father couldn’t soothe you, not even your brother could cheer you up.

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: What happened to that little boy?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I guess he just grew up and learned not everything can be solved by a hug.

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: I guess he did.

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: Parental authority

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: LINH used PARENTAL  AUTHORITY

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: It’s not very effective

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: Do not talk to your own mother like this.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I’ll do what I want when I’m right.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: A little cursing is the least you deserve from me.

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: I really was too lenient on you.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Disappointment

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: LINH used DISAPPOINTMENT

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: I really did expect better of you, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: I thought you would become a kinder, more understanding and mature person.

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: Maybe I expected too much.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Comparison

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: LINH used COMPARISON

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: God, I never got this much of a headache talking to Caspian at your age.

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: Why can’t you just… be a little more like him.

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: He’s always been so much easier to talk to.

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: In the past five years at least.

dialogue.speaker: Linh
dialogue.text: Can’t you just learn a little from your brother.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Wow, thanks mum. That really endeared me to your plight.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: I’m sorry. Is that what you want me to say?

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: I’m sorry my choices impacted you and it hurt your relationship with your dad.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Hurt my relationship?

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: He hasn’t talked to me in five years.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: He found out I wasn’t his real son and walked out the very same day!

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Because you cheated!

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: And you lied about it for fifteen years!

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: You lied to me.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: I didn’t know either…

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: You knew there was a possibility.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: And you had an affair for fifteen fucking years.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: It wasn’t like me and Noah were together all that time.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: It was a passing thing, back then, and we didn’t have any contact for years.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: We only started seeing each other seriously for about a year before your dad found out.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: Things with him were… different, than things with your father.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: It’s not an excuse, I know what I did back then was wrong.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: It was a messy time, back then.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: How was it messy? I don’t understand, I… I thought you guys were happy.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: Before you were born I had… a misscarriage. Two, technically.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: Twins.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: Turns out the survival rate of babies gets smaller the more there are.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: I was… devastated… to say the least.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: And your father was… not particularly… empathetic, in the aftermath.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: Perhaps it was just the difference between carrying the pregnancy and not,

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: To your father the twins were more of a concept than a pair of actual children. I was… more connected, not just in the physical sense.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I… didn’t know that happened.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: It sounds… terrible.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: It was a long time ago. And not something I particularly like talking about.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: I met Noah while I was still grieving.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: And he was kind, sympathetic. A very gentle man.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: The opposite of your father, in some ways.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: I was young when I married your father.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: Even younger when he brought Caspian back and I became a mother.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: Part of me just wanted to explore, I think.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Seems like you still like him now, though.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I guess things were more complicated than they seemed at first…

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: But…

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: You still cheated.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: And Noah knew that too.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I… don’t know if I can look past that…

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: I suppose it’s too much to ask you to fully understand.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: You’re not married and you haven’t got any kids, let alone lost any.

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: You’re twenty now, same age as I was when I met your father,

intermission-50%.speaker: Linh
intermission-50%.text: but in the end, you’re still a child.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: He didn’t ask me about Caspian, you know.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: It’s not like we expected his real parents to die in that crash, but your father didn’t ask me before he brought Caspian back to stay with us.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: Then suddenly I was twenty-two, trying to raise a surprise baby that wasn’t my own.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: …not that I regret it.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: Caspian is my son, no matter what, but…

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: When your father proposed to me two years later…

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: I barely even thought about it.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: Not because I knew I loved your father, but because… we had a child.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: My mother once said I was the only idiot stupid enough to get baby-trapped without even actually having a kid.

win.speaker: You
win.text: Did you ever actually love dad?

win.speaker: You
win.text: Or did we just… keep you there.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: You didn’t do anything. Neither did Caspian.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: I’m an adult woman that made my own choices based on the things I valued most.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: And your father…

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: I’m sure I loved him at some point.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: Or at least something close enough to love.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: I was twenty and killing myself in med-school, trying to keep my scholarship and get ahead of my classes.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: I didn’t have the connection in the field my peers had, I was fluent in English but it felt like my accent was so noticeable. I felt out of place, still, even after seven years in the country.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: Your father was… confident.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: Self-assured and charismatic. He was a handsome, charming man and people were always trying to impress him, but he was hard to impress.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: It felt like I was… proving something, when he asked me out.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: Like I was better than all those insecure thoughts.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: He made me feel confident in myself, I guess.

win.speaker: You
win.text: That’s a good thing, isn’t it?

win.speaker: You
win.text: Why do you sound so sad?

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: It was a false confidence, in the end. A borrowed confidence.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: I was so reliant on his opinion of me to tell me how to view myself.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: It took me a while to untangle it all. My time with Noah helped me figure myself out in a place that didn’t involve him.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: It’s something I think you might benefit from.

win.speaker: You
win.text: What? Meeting Noah?

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: Distancing yourself from your father.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: And I know he hasn’t talked to you in years, but I mean distancing your sense of self.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I know who I am without dad, thank you very much.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I had to figure it out.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: I don’t know if you do…

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: All your relationships with your family seem to revolve around him somehow anyway.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: Even when he’s gone…

win.speaker: You
win.text: That’s not…

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: Meeting with Noah isn’t going to replace your father.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: You don’t have to build that kind of relationship with him.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: But I worry…

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: …I worry that the reason you don’t want to meet him isn’t because you yourself don’t want to,

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: But because you think its somehow betraying your father.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: …but he left you.

win.speaker: Linh
win.text: You can’t betray someone who’s not even part of your life anymore.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I’ll… I’ll think about it.

win.speaker: You
win.text: No promise, but… I’ll actually think about it.

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: Self-centred? Ungrateful? From the woman who had an affair, destroyed our whole family and ruined my life.

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: Mother of the year over here!
